<skill_content name="pptx">
# Skill: pptx

# PPTX Skill

## Quick Reference

| Task                         | Guide                                    |
| ---------------------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| Read/analyze content         | `python -m markitdown presentation.pptx` |
| Edit or create from template | Read [editing.md](editing.md)            |
| Create from scratch          | Read [pptxgenjs.md](pptxgenjs.md)        |

---

## Reading Content

```bash
# Text extraction
python -m markitdown presentation.pptx

# Visual overview
python scripts/thumbnail.py presentation.pptx

# Raw XML
python scripts/office/unpack.py presentation.pptx unpacked/
```

---

## Editing Workflow

**Read [editing.md](editing.md) for full details.**

1. Analyze template with `thumbnail.py`
2. Unpack → manipulate slides → edit content → clean → pack

---

## Creating from Scratch

**Read [pptxgenjs.md](pptxgenjs.md) for full details.**

Use when no template or reference presentation is available.

---

## Design Ideas

**Don't create boring slides.** Plain bullets on a white background won't impress anyone. Consider ideas from this list for each slide.

### Before Starting

- **Pick a bold, content-informed color palette**: The palette should feel designed for THIS topic. If swapping your colors into a completely different presentation would still "work," you haven't made specific enough choices.
- **Dominance over equality**: One color should dominate (60-70% visual weight), with 1-2 supporting tones and one sharp accent. Never give all colors equal weight.
- **Dark/light contrast**: Dark backgrounds for title + conclusion slides, light for content ("sandwich" structure). Or commit to dark throughout for a premium feel.
- **Commit to a visual motif**: Pick ONE distinctive element and repeat it — rounded image frames, icons in colored circles, thick single-side borders. Carry it across every slide.

### Color Palettes

Choose colors that match your topic — don't default to generic blue. Use these palettes as inspiration:

| Theme                  | Primary               | Secondary             | Accent              |
| ---------------------- | --------------------- | --------------------- | ------------------- |
| **Midnight Executive** | `1E2761` (navy)       | `CADCFC` (ice blue)   | `FFFFFF` (white)    |
| **Forest & Moss**      | `2C5F2D` (forest)     | `97BC62` (moss)       | `F5F5F5` (cream)    |
| **Coral Energy**       | `F96167` (coral)      | `F9E795` (gold)       | `2F3C7E` (navy)     |
| **Warm Terracotta**    | `B85042` (terracotta) | `E7E8D1` (sand)       | `A7BEAE` (sage)     |
| **Ocean Gradient**     | `065A82` (deep blue)  | `1C7293` (teal)       | `21295C` (midnight) |
| **Charcoal Minimal**   | `36454F` (charcoal)   | `F2F2F2` (off-white)  | `212121` (black)    |
| **Teal Trust**         | `028090` (teal)       | `00A896` (seafoam)    | `02C39A` (mint)     |
| **Berry & Cream**      | `6D2E46` (berry)      | `A26769` (dusty rose) | `ECE2D0` (cream)    |
| **Sage Calm**          | `84B59F` (sage)       | `69A297` (eucalyptus) | `50808E` (slate)    |
| **Cherry Bold**        | `990011` (cherry)     | `FCF6F5` (off-white)  | `2F3C7E` (navy)     |

### For Each Slide

**Every slide needs a visual element** — image, chart, icon, or shape. Text-only slides are forgettable.

**Layout options:**

- Two-column (text left, illustration on right)
- Icon + text rows (icon in colored circle, bold header, description below)
- 2x2 or 2x3 grid (image on one side, grid of content blocks on other)
- Half-bleed image (full left or right side) with content overlay

**Data display:**

- Large stat callouts (big numbers 60-72pt with small labels below)
- Comparison columns (before/after, pros/cons, side-by-side options)
- Timeline or process flow (numbered steps, arrows)

**Visual polish:**

- Icons in small colored circles next to section headers
- Italic accent text for key stats or taglines

### Typography

**Choose an interesting font pairing** — don't default to Arial. Pick a header font with personality and pair it with a clean body font.

| Header Font  | Body Font     |
| ------------ | ------------- |
| Georgia      | Calibri       |
| Arial Black  | Arial         |
| Calibri      | Calibri Light |
| Cambria      | Calibri       |
| Trebuchet MS | Calibri       |
| Impact       | Arial         |
| Palatino     | Garamond      |
| Consolas     | Calibri       |

| Element        | Size          |
| -------------- | ------------- |
| Slide title    | 36-44pt bold  |
| Section header | 20-24pt bold  |
| Body text      | 14-16pt       |
| Captions       | 10-12pt muted |

### Spacing

- 0.5" minimum margins
- 0.3-0.5" between content blocks
- Leave breathing room—don't fill every inch

### Avoid (Common Mistakes)

- **Don't repeat the same layout** — vary columns, cards, and callouts across slides
- **Don't center body text** — left-align paragraphs and lists; center only titles
- **Don't skimp on size contrast** — titles need 36pt+ to stand out from 14-16pt body
- **Don't default to blue** — pick colors that reflect the specific topic
- **Don't mix spacing randomly** — choose 0.3" or 0.5" gaps and use consistently
- **Don't style one slide and leave the rest plain** — commit fully or keep it simple throughout
- **Don't create text-only slides** — add images, icons, charts, or visual elements; avoid plain title + bullets
- **Don't forget text box padding** — when aligning lines or shapes with text edges, set `margin: 0` on the text box or offset the shape to account for padding
- **Don't use low-contrast elements** — icons AND text need strong contrast against the background; avoid light text on light backgrounds or dark text on dark backgrounds
- **NEVER use accent lines under titles** — these are a hallmark of AI-generated slides; use whitespace or background color instead

---

## QA (Required)

**Assume there are problems. Your job is to find them.**

Your first render is almost never correct. Approach QA as a bug hunt, not a confirmation step. If you found zero issues on first inspection, you weren't looking hard enough.

### Content QA

```bash
python -m markitdown output.pptx
```

Check for missing content, typos, wrong order.

**When using templates, check for leftover placeholder text:**

```bash
python -m markitdown output.pptx | grep -iE "xxxx|lorem|ipsum|this.*(page|slide).*layout"
```

If grep returns results, fix them before declaring success.

### Visual QA

**⚠️ USE SUBAGENTS** — even for 2-3 slides. You've been staring at the code and will see what you expect, not what's there. Subagents have fresh eyes.

Convert slides to images (see [Converting to Images](#converting-to-images)), then use this prompt:

```
Visually inspect these slides. Assume there are issues — find them.

Look for:
- Overlapping elements (text through shapes, lines through words, stacked elements)
- Text overflow or cut off at edges/box boundaries
- Decorative lines positioned for single-line text but title wrapped to two lines
- Source citations or footers colliding with content above
- Elements too close (< 0.3" gaps) or cards/sections nearly touching
- Uneven gaps (large empty area in one place, cramped in another)
- Insufficient margin from slide edges (< 0.5")
- Columns or similar elements not aligned consistently
- Low-contrast text (e.g., light gray text on cream-colored background)
- Low-contrast icons (e.g., dark icons on dark backgrounds without a contrasting circle)
- Text boxes too narrow causing excessive wrapping
- Leftover placeholder content

For each slide, list issues or areas of concern, even if minor.

Read and analyze these images:
1. /path/to/slide-01.jpg (Expected: [brief description])
2. /path/to/slide-02.jpg (Expected: [brief description])

Report ALL issues found, including minor ones.
```

### Verification Loop

1. Generate slides → Convert to images → Inspect
2. **List issues found** (if none found, look again more critically)
3. Fix issues
4. **Re-verify affected slides** — one fix often creates another problem
5. Repeat until a full pass reveals no new issues

**Do not declare success until you've completed at least one fix-and-verify cycle.**

---

## Converting to Images

Convert presentations to individual slide images for visual inspection:

```bash
python scripts/office/soffice.py --headless --convert-to pdf output.pptx
pdftoppm -jpeg -r 150 output.pdf slide
```

This creates `slide-01.jpg`, `slide-02.jpg`, etc.

To re-render specific slides after fixes:

```bash
pdftoppm -jpeg -r 150 -f N -l N output.pdf slide-fixed
```

---

## Dependencies

- `pip install "markitdown[pptx]"` - text extraction
- `pip install Pillow` - thumbnail grids
- `npm install -g pptxgenjs` - creating from scratch
- LibreOffice (`soffice`) - PDF conversion (auto-configured for sandboxed environments via `scripts/office/soffice.py`)
- Poppler (`pdftoppm`) - PDF to images

"""
Script to normalize PowerPoint presentation:
1. Count slides with title font size NOT equal to 36pt
2. Normalize all slide titles to Arial, Bold, 36pt
3. Add footer to every slide: 'Confidential — {{SlideNumber}} of 12'
4. Move slide 7 to become slide 2
5. Save result
"""

from pptx import Presentation
from pptx.util import Pt
from copy import deepcopy
import os

# File paths
input_file = "uploads/deck_messy.pptx"
output_file = "outputs/deck_normalized.pptx"

# Ensure output directory exists
os.makedirs("outputs", exist_ok=True)

# Load presentation
prs = Presentation(input_file)

# Task 1: Count slides with title font size NOT equal to 36pt
slides_with_wrong_size = 0
title_sizes_before = []

for slide_idx, slide in enumerate(prs.slides):
    # Try to find title shape
    title_shape = None
    for shape in slide.shapes:
        if hasattr(shape, "text") and shape.has_text_frame:
            # Check if it's likely a title (usually the first text shape or has title in placeholder)
            if hasattr(shape, "placeholder_format"):
                try:
                    if shape.placeholder_format.type == 1:  # Title placeholder type
                        title_shape = shape
                        break
                except:
                    pass
    
    # If no placeholder found, use first text shape
    if not title_shape:
        for shape in slide.shapes:
            if hasattr(shape, "text_frame") and shape.has_text_frame:
                title_shape = shape
                break
    
    if title_shape and title_shape.has_text_frame:
        # Check font size of the first run in the title
        if title_shape.text_frame.paragraphs:
            para = title_shape.text_frame.paragraphs[0]
            if para.runs:
                run = para.runs[0]
                current_size = run.font.size
                if current_size:
                    size_pt = current_size.pt
                    title_sizes_before.append((slide_idx + 1, size_pt))
                    if size_pt != 36:
                        slides_with_wrong_size += 1
                else:
                    # No explicit size set, check paragraph level
                    title_sizes_before.append((slide_idx + 1, "Not set"))
                    slides_with_wrong_size += 1

print(f"Slides with wrong title size (before normalization): {slides_with_wrong_size}")
print(f"Title sizes found: {title_sizes_before}")

# Task 2: Normalize all slide titles to Arial, Bold, 36pt
titles_normalized = 0

for slide_idx, slide in enumerate(prs.slides):
    # Find title shape
    title_shape = None
    for shape in slide.shapes:
        if hasattr(shape, "placeholder_format"):
            try:
                if shape.placeholder_format.type == 1:  # Title placeholder
                    title_shape = shape
                    break
            except:
                pass
    
    # If no placeholder, use first text shape
    if not title_shape:
        for shape in slide.shapes:
            if hasattr(shape, "text_frame") and shape.has_text_frame:
                title_shape = shape
                break
    
    if title_shape and title_shape.has_text_frame:
        for paragraph in title_shape.text_frame.paragraphs:
            for run in paragraph.runs:
                run.font.name = "Arial"
                run.font.bold = True
                run.font.size = Pt(36)
        titles_normalized += 1

print(f"Titles normalized: {titles_normalized}")

# Task 3: Add footer to every slide
footer_added = 0
total_slides = len(prs.slides)

for slide_idx, slide in enumerate(prs.slides):
    slide_number = slide_idx + 1
    footer_text = f"Confidential — {slide_number} of {total_slides}"
    
    # Add text box for footer at bottom of slide
    # Slide dimensions in inches (standard is 10" x 7.5")
    from pptx.util import Inches
    
    # Position footer at bottom center
    left = Inches(1)
    top = Inches(7)
    width = Inches(8)
    height = Inches(0.4)
    
    textbox = slide.shapes.add_textbox(left, top, width, height)
    text_frame = textbox.text_frame
    text_frame.text = footer_text
    
    # Format footer text
    paragraph = text_frame.paragraphs[0]
    paragraph.alignment = 1  # Center alignment
    run = paragraph.runs[0]
    run.font.size = Pt(10)
    run.font.name = "Arial"
    
    footer_added += 1

print(f"Footers added: {footer_added}")

# Task 4: Move slide 7 to position 2 (before the reordering)
# In python-pptx, we need to manipulate the XML structure
# The slides are stored in prs.slides._sldIdLst

slide_to_move_idx = 6  # 0-indexed (slide 7)
new_position_idx = 1    # 0-indexed (position 2)

# Get the slide ID list from the XML
slides_list = prs.slides._sldIdLst
slide_elements = list(slides_list)

# Move slide 7 to position 2
if slide_to_move_idx < len(slide_elements) and new_position_idx < len(slide_elements):
    # Remove the element at slide 7
    slide_to_move = slide_elements[slide_to_move_idx]
    slides_list.remove(slide_to_move)
    
    # Insert it at position 2
    slides_list.insert(new_position_idx, slide_to_move)
    
    print(f"Slide 7 moved to position 2")
    slide_reordered = True
else:
    print(f"Could not move slide - index out of range")
    slide_reordered = False

# Now we need to re-add footers with correct numbers after reordering
# Remove all footers and re-add with correct numbers
for slide in prs.slides:
    shapes_to_remove = []
    for shape in slide.shapes:
        if hasattr(shape, "text") and "Confidential —" in shape.text:
            shapes_to_remove.append(shape)
    
    for shape in shapes_to_remove:
        sp = shape.element
        sp.getparent().remove(sp)

# Re-add footers with correct numbers
footer_added = 0
total_slides = len(prs.slides)

for slide_idx, slide in enumerate(prs.slides):
    slide_number = slide_idx + 1
    footer_text = f"Confidential — {slide_number} of {total_slides}"
    
    from pptx.util import Inches
    
    left = Inches(1)
    top = Inches(7)
    width = Inches(8)
    height = Inches(0.4)
    
    textbox = slide.shapes.add_textbox(left, top, width, height)
    text_frame = textbox.text_frame
    text_frame.text = footer_text
    
    paragraph = text_frame.paragraphs[0]
    paragraph.alignment = 1
    run = paragraph.runs[0]
    run.font.size = Pt(10)
    run.font.name = "Arial"
    
    footer_added += 1

# Save the presentation
prs.save(output_file)
print(f"\nPresentation saved to: {output_file}")

# Final results
print("\n" + "="*50)
print("FINAL RESULTS:")
print(f"SlidesWithWrongTitleSize: {slides_with_wrong_size}")
print(f"TitlesNormalized: {titles_normalized}")
print(f"FooterAddedToSlides: {footer_added}")
print(f"SlideReordered: {'Yes' if slide_reordered else 'No'}")
print("="*50)

"""
Detailed analysis of title formatting in the messy deck
"""

from pptx import Presentation
from pptx.util import Pt

input_file = "uploads/deck_messy.pptx"

prs = Presentation(input_file)

print(f"Total slides: {len(prs.slides)}\n")

for slide_idx, slide in enumerate(prs.slides, 1):
    print(f"=== SLIDE {slide_idx} ===")
    
    # Look at all shapes
    for shape_idx, shape in enumerate(slide.shapes):
        if hasattr(shape, "text_frame") and shape.has_text_frame:
            print(f"  Shape {shape_idx}: '{shape.text[:50]}...' " if len(shape.text) > 50 else f"  Shape {shape_idx}: '{shape.text}'")
            
            # Check if it's a placeholder
            is_title = False
            if hasattr(shape, "placeholder_format"):
                try:
                    placeholder_type = shape.placeholder_format.type
                    print(f"    Placeholder type: {placeholder_type}")
                    if placeholder_type == 1:
                        is_title = True
                        print(f"    --> THIS IS A TITLE PLACEHOLDER")
                except:
                    pass
            
            # Check font properties
            if shape.text_frame.paragraphs:
                for para_idx, para in enumerate(shape.text_frame.paragraphs):
                    if para.runs:
                        for run_idx, run in enumerate(para.runs):
                            font_name = run.font.name
                            font_size = run.font.size
                            font_bold = run.font.bold
                            
                            size_pt = font_size.pt if font_size else "Not set"
                            
                            print(f"      Para {para_idx}, Run {run_idx}:")
                            print(f"        Font: {font_name}")
                            print(f"        Size: {size_pt}")
                            print(f"        Bold: {font_bold}")
                            print(f"        Text: '{run.text}'")
    print()

"""
Detailed analysis of title formatting in the messy deck (fixed version)
"""

from pptx import Presentation
from pptx.util import Pt

input_file = "uploads/deck_messy.pptx"

prs = Presentation(input_file)

print(f"Total slides: {len(prs.slides)}\n")

for slide_idx, slide in enumerate(prs.slides, 1):
    print(f"=== SLIDE {slide_idx} ===")
    
    # Look at all shapes
    for shape_idx, shape in enumerate(slide.shapes):
        if hasattr(shape, "text_frame") and shape.has_text_frame:
            print(f"  Shape {shape_idx}: '{shape.text[:50]}...' " if len(shape.text) > 50 else f"  Shape {shape_idx}: '{shape.text}'")
            
            # Check if it's a placeholder - use try/except to avoid errors
            is_title = False
            try:
                if hasattr(shape, "placeholder_format"):
                    placeholder_type = shape.placeholder_format.type
                    print(f"    Placeholder type: {placeholder_type}")
                    if placeholder_type == 1:
                        is_title = True
                        print(f"    --> THIS IS A TITLE PLACEHOLDER")
            except ValueError:
                # Not a placeholder
                pass
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"    Error checking placeholder: {e}")
            
            # Check font properties

input.content:
"""
Script to properly analyze and normalize PowerPoint presentation:
This version properly handles shape types and title identification
"""

from pptx import Presentation
from pptx.util import Pt, Inches
from pptx.enum.text import PP_ALIGN
import os

# File paths
input_file = "uploads/deck_messy.pptx"
output_file = "outputs/deck_normalized.pptx"

# Ensure output directory exists
os.makedirs("outputs", exist_ok=True)

# Load presentation
prs = Presentation(input_file)

print(f"Analyzing presentation with {len(prs.slides)} slides\n")

# Task 1: Count slides with title font size NOT equal to 36pt
# We need to check the actual rendered size, which may come from theme
slides_with_wrong_size = 0
title_info = []

def get_effective_font_size(run, para):
    """Get the effective font size, checking run, paragraph, and defaults"""
    if run.font.size:
        return run.font.size.pt
    # If not set at run level, it inherits from theme - we need to check the master
    # For this analysis, if size is not explicitly set, we'll consider it as "wrong"
    return None

for slide_idx, slide in enumerate(prs.slides, 1):
    # Find the title shape - it's typically Shape 1 based on our analysis
    title_shape = None
    title_text = ""
    
    # First try to find by placeholder type
    for shape in slide.shapes:
        if shape.has_text_frame:
            try:
                if hasattr(shape, "placeholder_format") and shape.placeholder_format.type == 1:
                    title_shape = shape
                    break
            except:
                pass
    
    # If not found, use the first non-empty text shape
    if not title_shape:
        for shape in slide.shapes:
            if shape.has_text_frame and shape.text.strip():
                title_shape = shape
                title_text = shape.text
                break
    
    if title_shape and title_shape.text_frame.paragraphs:
        para = title_shape.text_frame.paragraphs[0]
        if para.runs:
            run = para.runs[0]
            font_name = run.font.name or "Not set"
            font_size_pt = get_effective_font_size(run, para)
            is_bold = run.font.bold
            
            title_info.append({
                'slide': slide_idx,
                'text': title_shape.text[:30],
                'font': font_name,
                'size': font_size_pt,
                'bold': is_bold
            })
            
            # Count as wrong if not 36pt
            if font_size_pt != 36:
                slides_with_wrong_size += 1
                print(f"Slide {slide_idx}: Title '{title_shape.text[:30]}' - Size: {font_size_pt}pt (WRONG)")
            else:
                print(f"Slide {slide_idx}: Title '{title_shape.text[:30]}' - Size: {font_size_pt}pt (OK)")

print(f"\n{'='*60}")
print(f"Count: {slides_with_wrong_size} slides with title size NOT equal to 36pt")
print(f"{'='*60}\n")

# Reload for fresh modification
prs = Presentation(input_file)

# Task 2: Normalize all slide titles to Arial, Bold, 36pt
titles_normalized = 0

for slide_idx, slide in enumerate(prs.slides, 1):
    # Find title shape
    title_shape = None
    
    # First try placeholder
    for shape in slide.shapes:
        if shape.has_text_frame:
            try:
                if hasattr(shape, "placeholder_format") and shape.placeholder_format.type == 1:
                    title_shape = shape
                    break
            except:
                pass
    
    # If not found, use first non-empty text shape
    if not title_shape:
        for shape in slide.shapes:
            if shape.has_text_frame and shape.text.strip():
                title_shape = shape
                break
    
    if title_shape:
        for paragraph in title_shape.text_frame.paragraphs:
            for run in paragraph.runs:
                run.font.name = "Arial"
                run.font.bold = True
                run.font.size = Pt(36)
        titles_normalized += 1
        print(f"Normalized title on slide {slide_idx}")

print(f"\n{'='*60}")
print(f"Normalized: {titles_normalized} titles")
print(f"{'='*60}\n")

# Task 4: Move slide 7 to position 2 (do this before adding footers)
print("Moving slide 7 to position 2...")
slides_list = prs.slides._sldIdLst
slide_elements = list(slides_list)

slide_to_move_idx = 6  # 0-indexed (slide 7)
new_position_idx = 1    # 0-indexed (position 2)

slide_reordered = False
if slide_to_move_idx < len(slide_elements):
    slide_to_move = slide_elements[slide_to_move_idx]
    slides_list.remove(slide_to_move)
    slides_list.insert(new_position_idx, slide_to_move)
    slide_reordered = True
    print("Slide 7 moved to position 2")
else:
    print("Could not move slide - index out of range")

print(f"{'='*60}\n")

# Task 3: Add footer to every slide (after reordering)
footer_added = 0
total_slides = len(prs.slides)

print(f"Adding footers to {total_slides} slides...")

for slide_idx, slide in enumerate(prs.slides, 1):
    footer_text = f"Confidential — {slide_idx} of {total_slides}"
    
    # Position footer at bottom center
    left = Inches(1)
    top = Inches(7)
    width = Inches(8)
    height = Inches(0.4)
    
    textbox = slide.shapes.add_textbox(left, top, width, height)
    text_frame = textbox.text_frame
    text_frame.text = footer_text
    
    # Format footer text
    paragraph = text_frame.paragraphs[0]
    paragraph.alignment = PP_ALIGN.CENTER
    run = paragraph.runs[0]
    run.font.size = Pt(10)
    run.font.name = "Arial"
    
    footer_added += 1
    print(f"  Added footer to slide {slide_idx}: '{footer_text}'")

print(f"\n{'='*60}")
print(f"Footer added to: {footer_added} slides")
print(f"{'='*60}\n")

# Save the presentation
prs.save(output_file)
print(f"Presentation saved to: {output_file}\n")

# Final results
print("="*60)
print("FINAL RESULTS:")
print("="*60)
print(f"SlidesWithWrongTitleSize: {slides_with_wrong_size}")
print(f"TitlesNormalized: {titles_normalized}")
print(f"FooterAddedToSlides: {footer_added}")
print(f"SlideReordered: {'Yes' if slide_reordered else 'No'}")
print("="*60)

"""
Deep dive into the actual XML structure to find real font sizes
"""

from pptx import Presentation
from pptx.util import Pt
from pptx.oxml import parse_xml
import zipfile
import os

input_file = "uploads/deck_messy.pptx"

# First, let's check using python-pptx shape properties
prs = Presentation(input_file)

print("="*70)
print("CHECKING TITLE FORMATTING BY EXAMINING SHAPES")
print("="*70)

count_wrong = 0

for slide_idx, slide in enumerate(prs.slides, 1):
    print(f"\n--- SLIDE {slide_idx} ---")
    
    # Find all text shapes
    for shape_idx, shape in enumerate(slide.shapes):
        if not shape.has_text_frame:
            continue
            
        text = shape.text.strip()
        if not text:
            continue
            
        # Check if this looks like a title (first substantial text shape)
        print(f"Shape {shape_idx}: {text[:40]}")
        
        # Get the XML element
        if shape.has_text_frame:
            tf = shape.text_frame
            for para in tf.paragraphs:
                # Check paragraph-level formatting
                if hasattr(para, '_element'):
                    para_elem = para._element
                    # Look for pPr (paragraph properties) -> defRPr (default run properties)
                    pPr = para_elem.find('.//{http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/drawingml/2006/main}pPr')
                    if pPr is not None:
                        defRPr = pPr.find('.//{http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/drawingml/2006/main}defRPr')
                        if defRPr is not None and 'sz' in defRPr.attrib:
                            size_emu = int(defRPr.attrib['sz'])
                            size_pt = size_emu / 100
                            print(f"  Paragraph default size: {size_pt}pt")
                            if size_pt != 36:
                                count_wrong += 1
                                print(f"  ^^^ WRONG SIZE (expected 36pt)")
                
                for run in para.runs:
                    # Check run-level formatting
                    if hasattr(run, '_element'):
                        run_elem = run._element
                        rPr = run_elem.find('.//{http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/drawingml/2006/main}rPr')
                        if rPr is not None:
                            # Check size attribute (in EMUs, 1 pt = 100 EMUs)
                            if 'sz' in rPr.attrib:
                                size_emu = int(rPr.attrib['sz'])
                                size_pt = size_emu / 100
                                print(f"  Run size: {size_pt}pt")
                                
                            # Check font
                            latin = rPr.find('.//{http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/drawingml/2006/main}latin')
                            if latin is not None and 'typeface' in latin.attrib:
                                font_name = latin.attrib['typeface']
                                print(f"  Font: {font_name}")
                            
                            # Check bold
                            if 'b' in rPr.attrib:
                                is_bold = rPr.attrib['b'] == '1'
                                print(f"  Bold: {is_bold}")

print(f"\n{'='*70}")
print(f"Total slides with non-36pt titles based on XML: {count_wrong}")
print(f"{'='*70}")

# Now let's manually extract and examine the slide XML files

input.content:
"""
Final script to properly analyze and normalize PowerPoint presentation
This version correctly identifies only title shapes (Shape 1)
"""

from pptx import Presentation
from pptx.util import Pt, Inches
from pptx.enum.text import PP_ALIGN
import os

# File paths
input_file = "uploads/deck_messy.pptx"
output_file = "outputs/deck_normalized.pptx"

# Ensure output directory exists
os.makedirs("outputs", exist_ok=True)

# Load presentation
prs = Presentation(input_file)

print(f"Analyzing presentation with {len(prs.slides)} slides\n")

# Task 1: Count slides with title font size NOT equal to 36pt
# Title is Shape 1 (the first text shape after placeholder)
slides_with_wrong_size = 0
title_info = []

def get_paragraph_font_size(para):
    """Get font size from paragraph default run properties (XML level)"""
    if hasattr(para, '_element'):
        para_elem = para._element
        pPr = para_elem.find('.//{http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/drawingml/2006/main}pPr')
        if pPr is not None:
            defRPr = pPr.find('.//{http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/drawingml/2006/main}defRPr')
            if defRPr is not None and 'sz' in defRPr.attrib:
                size_emu = int(defRPr.attrib['sz'])
                size_pt = size_emu / 100
                return size_pt
    return None

for slide_idx, slide in enumerate(prs.slides, 1):
    # Find Shape 1 (the title shape)
    title_shape = None
    shape_counter = 0
    
    for shape in slide.shapes:
        if shape.has_text_frame and shape.text.strip():
            shape_counter += 1
            if shape_counter == 1:  # First text shape is the title
                title_shape = shape
                break
    
    if title_shape and title_shape.text_frame.paragraphs:
        para = title_shape.text_frame.paragraphs[0]
        size_pt = get_paragraph_font_size(para)
        
        title_text = title_shape.text.strip()
        print(f"Slide {slide_idx}: '{title_text}' - Size: {size_pt}pt", end="")
        
        if size_pt != 36:
            slides_with_wrong_size += 1
            print(" (WRONG)")
        else:
            print(" (OK)")

print(f"\n{'='*60}")
print(f"TASK 1: {slides_with_wrong_size} slides with title size NOT equal to 36pt")
print(f"{'='*60}\n")

# Reload for fresh modification
prs = Presentation(input_file)

# Task 2: Normalize all slide titles to Arial, Bold, 36pt
titles_normalized = 0

for slide_idx, slide in enumerate(prs.slides, 1):
    # Find Shape 1 (the title shape)
    title_shape = None
    shape_counter = 0
    
    for shape in slide.shapes:
        if shape.has_text_frame and shape.text.strip():
            shape_counter += 1
            if shape_counter == 1:  # First text shape is the title
                title_shape = shape
                break
    
    if title_shape:
        for paragraph in title_shape.text_frame.paragraphs:
            for run in paragraph.runs:
                run.font.name = "Arial"
                run.font.bold = True
                run.font.size = Pt(36)
        titles_normalized += 1

print(f"TASK 2: Normalized {titles_normalized} titles to Arial, Bold, 36pt\n")

# Task 4: Move slide 7 to position 2 (do this before adding footers)
print("TASK 4: Moving slide 7 to position 2...")
slides_list = prs.slides._sldIdLst
slide_elements = list(slides_list)

slide_to_move_idx = 6  # 0-indexed (slide 7)
new_position_idx = 1    # 0-indexed (position 2)

slide_reordered = False
if slide_to_move_idx < len(slide_elements):
    slide_to_move = slide_elements[slide_to_move_idx]
    slides_list.remove(slide_to_move)
    slides_list.insert(new_position_idx, slide_to_move)
    slide_reordered = True
    print("Slide 7 moved to position 2\n")
else:
    print("Could not move slide - index out of range\n")

# Task 3: Add footer to every slide (after reordering)
footer_added = 0
total_slides = len(prs.slides)

print(f"TASK 3: Adding footers to {total_slides} slides...")

for slide_idx, slide in enumerate(prs.slides, 1):
    footer_text = f"Confidential — {slide_idx} of {total_slides}"
    
    # Position footer at bottom center
    left = Inches(1)
    top = Inches(7)
    width = Inches(8)
    height = Inches(0.4)
    
    textbox = slide.shapes.add_textbox(left, top, width, height)
    text_frame = textbox.text_frame
    text_frame.text = footer_text
    
    # Format footer text
    paragraph = text_frame.paragraphs[0]
    paragraph.alignment = PP_ALIGN.CENTER
    run = paragraph.runs[0]
    run.font.size = Pt(10)
    run.font.name = "Arial"
    
    footer_added += 1

print(f"Added footer to {footer_added} slides\n")

# Save the presentation
prs.save(output_file)
print(f"Presentation saved to: {output_file}\n")

# Final results
print("="*60)
print("FINAL ANSWER:")
print("="*60)
print(f"SlidesWithWrongTitleSize: {slides_with_wrong_size}")
print(f"TitlesNormalized: {titles_normalized}")
print(f"FooterAddedToSlides: {footer_added}")
print(f"SlideReordered: {'Yes' if slide_reordered else 'No'}")
print("="*60)
